item.title: Definitely Spyware disguised under a new name
item.description: Ironically, most people don't know that windows 10 and 11 is already recording every keystroke you make. You can turn it off but it automatically turns itself back on every two weeks.
item.dc:creator: MissySassy

item.title: Steve Martin is a national treasure....
item.description: Steve Martin has a 12-year old daughter. He's kept her out of the public eye because he said "there's too much joy in the family life to compromise it in any way." So when, during the documentary of his life that aired on Apple TV, his daughter came in to say goodbye to him, they edited a stick-figure drawing of her that she drew so you couldn't see her.
item.dc:creator: AdmiralJohn

item.title: Bridges For Nanna
item.description:
This is my 18 year old blind and deaf dog Nanna We've been on an unusual mission the last month.
We moved to Minneapolis 2 months ago, it's been fun having all new places to explore with my dogs I realized pretty quickly that Nanna had a brand new love
Every time we were on a bridge over the Mississippi River, Nanna would stop in her tracks to look over the edge and feel the breeze on her face It was the most at peace I've ever seen her.
So I've made it my goal to bring Nanna to every bridge in Minneapolis and St Paul so she can feel the breeze
For some reason these Tweets started popping off I had never even had a fraction of those views before
So Nanna starred getting some attention and some really cool things have come from it
First it was the local news, this story will be sometime next month
Then it was the local NPR whose coming with us to the next bridge in a week And the biggest one, the one you probably all already know -
Keep and eye out for Nanna Pants as WeRateDogs features her to their 9 million followers! If she doesn't get a 1,000/10 - we riot at dawn!
As much as I love Nanna getting all this attention at 18 years old, it feels kinda funny that it's about this bridge thing Nanna has done way cooler stuff than this lol, but I'm really happy that people enjoy her adventures
If it encourages just one person in the world to someday adopt an old special needs dog then all the posts were worth it. 6 pedestrian friendly bridges down, about 20 more left to go!
I also wanted to take a moment to thank everyone whose been involved lately with TheFreePizzaDude, it feels like we've had a bit of a resurgence towards the end of year 4 More helpers, more upvoters, more people sending food and helping out with the Meal Fund contributions
We had our biggest post of the year, then our biggest grocery post, new Angels popping up on posts and more people upvoting I keep hoping the project doesn't just fade away and all you amazing people keep making sure that it doesn't I really appreciate it so much.
Nanna isn't just being dropped off near bridges and standing there, she's still doing some insane exercise Yesterday was her longest adventure ever! 7 miles, 18 years old, I'm just the proudest goddamn dog owner in the world.
I moved here because my parents are sick, but I could've never imagined the new life it would bring out of my dogs. Everyone is happy here, they're all crushing miles and I'd have to say we are closer as a family than we've ever been before. I'm excited for the next bridge Nanna Pants, get ready to feel the breeze on your beautiful eyeless face. ... TheFreePizzaDude tomorrow, Saturday at 1pm central! If you can't make it but would still like to help, you can contribute to the Imgurian Meal Fund and one of us will send the food for you! Anonymous Angels have been so huge for us, I love you all so much https://www.paypal.me/TheFreePizzaDude https://ko-fi.com/thefreepizzadude ... Ill be back soon with Nanna on the news and WeRateDogs! Hope you all have a beautiful weekend.
item.dc:creator: MrPuckett

item.title: Fresh(ish) Memes #37
item.dc:creator: ShaggyRoots

item.title: Woohoo! 3: UpDating
item.description:
I get it. My ex and I talked about trying again a few months ago, and we even dated briefly. Very briefly. Fuck! That should read "AGAIN," not "AGING."
"A phone call? Already? What if you think i sound like a Muppet?" "I'll be very disappointed if you dont sound like a Muppet!" So i called her and did my Kermit impression (it's actually really good). We talked, and laughed, and laughed, and laughed. This was a big deal for me. I have been learning to cope with social anxiety. Meeting new people used to terrify me, but I've made huge strides in that regard. I often struggle with verbal conversation, and have never been a big fan of phone conversations. But talking with her is easy.
During the course of our conversations, we both picked up on certain things that the other said, and each had our suspicions that we might have more in common than we were letting on. Turns out we have very similar upbringings, similar family situations, and understand each other in ways we probably wouldn't otherwise.
Did I mention that she is cute as hell?
item.dc:creator: ElbowDeepInASelfLovingOctopus

item.dc:creator: zeacorzeppelin10

item.title: It's The Same Old Same Old
item.description:
The pattern is exhausting no matter the industry or body at work. Food, healthcare, housing prices & rent, politics... The public notices something that isn't right and raises a fuss about it.
But of course the easiest and first thing that companies, politicians, etc. do is deny deny deny...
And the inevitably undisputable proof surfaces. And what will happen? The unfortunate likelihood is that even if prosecuted there will only be a fine. And that fine will probably be less than what was earned along the way so it then in effect just becomes the cost of doing business :-( Original article - https://www.theguardian.com/commentisfree/article/2024/may/23/corporations-price-gouging-big-oil
item.dc:creator: divjnky

item.title: One year in, and I still suck
item.description:
Hi guys, Kit here. The day I post this, it will be exactly one year since I began skateboarding. A lot has happened. Some good, some bad. I've hit a new chapter of my life now, one that I'm pretty excited about. In all honesty, I considered closing this account. I even wrote up a whole goodbye note. I just didn't think I could keep up with this, especially with the sheer amount of things I had going on in school. After about a day or two of deliberation, I decided to leave this up. And maybe I'll leave some things from my goodbye note here. I may not be a great skater, but I have honestly learned a lot within the past year. Firstly, it's okay to not be the best or to take a longer time with something. It took me a long time to recognize that because we are so used to seeing the sanitized and perfectly edited videos of the end result, not the struggle in the middle. It's the journey that matters. I want to skate because I enjoy doing it, not because I feel obligated to by the pressure of constant improvement. Secondly, no person is an island, especially in hobbies. Hobbies are made better by community. Go take a painting class, meet with people, and engage with others. It'll sure help you learn but also to create valuable friendships. I am super excited because the place I am moving has a huge skate park and meet ups with other people. And finally, I wanna thank all of the nice, kind, patient, and wonderful people I have met here. Ultimately, what made me take back my decision was going into my messages and reading all of the messages people sent about their journey in beginning to skate. Its an amazing hobby and has definitely made me a more active person. So I will stay, maybe updating about every two weeks, and looking forward to our online community.
This, was one year ago. Goooooooood this is so strange to look at. Long story short, I accidentally posted this publicly to Imgur while trying to get help on Reddit's NewSkaters page. I came back from my shift at work to discover that it had absolutely blown up and made it to the front page. It's crazy to me to look back at my very first posts compared to now. I feel like I was such a babbyyy back then! I had hair! Not much progress was made, but I just feel like such a different person compared to then!
I think I figured out an issue. Like some people said, I do tend to go overkill on going down when I begin my jump, but also my knees definitely don't come high enough when I'm without the chairs. My theory is that whatever muscles control that action suck (I don't know what they are, since I never took anatomy in high school. God its weird to say that). Luckily, I will have time to get my physical fitness in order!
Just a wee thing to get my kickturns a lot smoother. Its sort of scary riding backwards, so I push off of the wall and try to kickturn out of that situation. Y'all I am fr so excited about college. I got a couple things for my dorm, like a Talking Heads/David Byrne poster and a checkerboard rug. Hopefully I can figure out a way to store my records too. This is my first summer since I started high school that is completely free. I can finally get back into reading, playing chess, and music. So so so so so so excited.
item.dc:creator: KitSucksAtSkating
